req.title: Allergy intolerance
req.statement: A clinician shall record an AllergyIntolerance linked to the patient; subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-009 requires three verifiable behaviours: (1) a clinician can POST a valid AllergyIntolerance and receive 201 with the resource stored, (2) a subsequent MedicationRequest for a conflicting substance triggers a warning/alert, (3) posting an exact duplicate returns 409. I cover these with EP (happy-path POST), STC (state transition: allergy recorded → MedicationRequest conflict alert fired), NEG (duplicate 409), BVA (minimum-viable payload with only required FHIR fields), and DT (conflicting vs non-conflicting medication). Five tests in total satisfy the ≥3 requirement while achieving broad coverage.

tests.name: test_post_valid_allergy_returns_201_and_stores_resource
preconditions: Clinician auth token available via <clinician_token> fixture
No prior allergy for this patient+substance combination
actions: Build a well-formed AllergyIntolerance payload referencing the patient
Capture response status and Location header
expected: GET on returned Location returns the same resource

tests.name: test_medication_request_for_allergen_triggers_conflict_warning
preconditions: Clinician auth token available via <clinician_token> fixture
AllergyIntolerance for substance S already stored (state: allergy-recorded)
actions: POST a MedicationRequest referencing the same substance S for the patient
Inspect response body for warnings or decision-support issues array
expected: Response body or X-Warnings header contains at least one allergy-conflict alert
Alert references the stored AllergyIntolerance id
trace: R-HLT-009: subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_medication_request_for_allergen_triggers_conflict_warning(
    base_url, clinician_token, patient_id, recorded_allergy
):
    # recorded_allergy fixture: dict with keys 'id' and 'substance_code'
    substance_code = recorded_allergy["substance_code"]
    med_payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct",
                        "code": substance_code}]
        }
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest",
        json=med_payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    warnings = (
        body.get("issue") or
        body.get("warnings") or
        body.get("decisionSupport") or []
    )
    assert len(warnings) > 0, "Expected at least one conflict warning"
    warning_text = str(warnings)
    assert recorded_allergy["id"] in warning_text or \
        substance_code in warning_text, \
        "Warning must reference the allergen or AllergyIntolerance id"

tests.name: test_duplicate_allergy_post_returns_409
preconditions: Clinician auth token available via <clinician_token> fixture
Identical AllergyIntolerance already present for this patient+substance
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with the exact same patient+substance payload a second time
expected: Response body indicates duplicate resource error
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_duplicate_allergy_post_returns_409(
    base_url, clinician_token, patient_id, recorded_allergy
):
    substance_code = recorded_allergy["substance_code"]
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct",
                              "code": substance_code}]},
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"code": "active"}]},
        "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"code": "confirmed"}]}
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409, (
        f"Expected 409, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome" or \
        "duplicate" in str(body).lower() or \
        "conflict" in str(body).lower(), \
        "Response should describe a duplicate/conflict error"

tests.name: test_minimum_required_fields_allergy_accepted
preconditions: Clinician auth token available via <clinician_token> fixture
No prior allergy record for this patient+substance
actions: Build an AllergyIntolerance payload containing ONLY the mandatory FHIR R4 fields (patient + code)
expected: Returned resource contains the submitted patient reference and code

tests.name: test_non_conflicting_medication_produces_no_allergy_warning
preconditions: Clinician auth token available via <clinician_token> fixture
AllergyIntolerance for substance S already stored
A different substance T with no recorded allergy is available
actions: POST a MedicationRequest for substance T (no allergy on record)
expected: Response contains zero allergy-conflict warnings related to substance T
trace: R-HLT-009: alerts regenerated only when conflict exists
